message:
fix(quic): improve TLS certificate configuration in client endpoint

Refactors client endpoint TLS setup for better certificate handling:

Root certificate store:
- Use provided root_certs from config if available
- Fall back to native roots (if tls-native-roots feature enabled)
- Extend with WebPKI roots for comprehensive coverage
- Fail with clear error if no roots available and verify enabled

Certificate verification:
- Only skip verification if config.insecure_skip_verify is true
- Proper certificate chain validation when verify is enabled
- Clear error message explaining how to configure certificates

Error handling:
- Map checkpoint cancellation to QuicError::Cancelled
- Return QuicError::Config for certificate configuration errors
- Return QuicError::TlsConfig for native root loading failures

This makes the QUIC client secure by default while allowing escape
hatches for testing (insecure_skip_verify) and custom CA deployments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
